summary: Diagnosed the blocker without launching training. The active shell Python is missing the ML stack, but the existing `.venv-bench-py311/bin/python` has the required packages and CUDA-enabled PyTorch. CUDA still fails there: `nvidia-smi` exits 9, `/dev/nvidia0` and `/dev/nvidiactl` are absent, `nvidia-modprobe -u -c=0` exits 1, and PyTorch sees zero CUDA devices. This is an operator-side GPU device-node/driver initialization blocker, not a Python package reinstall issue. I updated the diagnostic classifier and CPU smoke test so missing active-shell PyTorch is no longer mislabeled as a CPU-only wheel.
commands_run: for d in results/_jobs/*; do ... inspect command/status/exit/log ...; done
probe active python, venv candidates, nvidia-smi, /dev/nvidia*, /proc/driver/nvidia/version, lsmod, lspci
next_action: Operator-side VM stop/start or reboot, then rerun the official Google GPU driver installer if `nvidia-smi` still fails. After that, verify `nvidia-smi` and PyTorch CUDA visibility from `.venv-bench-py311/bin/python`, then rerun only the infrastructure gate.
